$comment: Allowlist for scripts/audit-gate.mjs — the reachability-based security gate.
An entry here SILENCES a high/critical advisory. That is only ever
acceptable for one of exactly TWO reasons, recorded in `category`:
unreachable  — the package is dev/build tooling and does not execute in
the deployed API or web runtime.
pin-locked   — the advisory IS reachable, but the published fix breaks a
deliberate version pin recorded in CLAUDE.md. `pin` names
NEVER allowlist something that is both reachable and fixable — fix it.
`paths` (optional) narrows an entry to specific node_modules locations.
Use it whenever only a nested copy is affected, so the gate still fails if
`tracked` marks an entry we intend to remove; the gate prints these on
every run so they cannot quietly become permanent.

allow.reason: unreachable: dev/build tooling, not in deployed API/web (expo > @expo/cli). In-range fix pending in the deps-hardening unit.

unreachable: dev/build tooling, not in deployed API/web. Reached only via firebase-admin > @firebase/database-compat; push.service.ts uses messaging() only and never opens a Realtime Database socket. In-range fix pending in the deps-hardening unit.

unreachable: dev/build tooling, not in deployed API/web in the exploitable sense — reached only via twilio, which builds its own requests; no attacker-controlled axios config or hostile multipart is ever parsed. In-range fix pending in the deps-hardening unit.
unreachable: dev/build tooling, not in deployed API/web (glob/minimatch inside eslint, jest, metro, rimraf). In-range fix pending in the deps-hardening unit.
unreachable: dev/build tooling, not in deployed API/web (@nestjs/cli > @angular-devkit/core; @nestjs/cli is a devDependency). In-range fix pending in the deps-hardening unit.
unreachable: dev/build tooling, not in deployed API/web (react-native > react-devtools-core). In-range fix pending in the deps-hardening unit.
unreachable: dev/build tooling, not in deployed API/web. Fix is expo@57, which breaks the Expo SDK 54 pin in CLAUDE.md.
unreachable: dev/build tooling, not in deployed API/web. Fix is expo@57, which breaks the Expo SDK 54 pin.
unreachable: dev/build tooling, not in deployed API/web. Fix is expo@57, which breaks the Expo SDK 54 pin.

unreachable: dev/build tooling, not in deployed API/web. The prod path is @nestjs/swagger, but main.ts only mounts SwaggerModule when NODE_ENV !== 'production'. Deliberately NOT bumped: the fix version 5.2.1 is itself high-severity (GHSA-pm4m-ph32-ghv5).

fix breaks a deliberate pin: npm's remedy is next@14.2.35, a major downgrade. Already on the latest patch of the pinned line (15.5.21), which cleared the 8 Server-Action/cache-confusion advisories. The residual entry is next's transitive postcss + its own nested sharp.

fix breaks a deliberate pin: this is next's PRIVATE nested copy (0.34.5), remediable only by downgrading to next@14. Our own direct dependency is sharp 0.35.3 and is NOT flagged. Path-scoped on purpose so the gate still fails if node_modules/sharp regresses.

fix breaks a deliberate pin: clearing GHSA-r28c-9q8g-f849 needs postcss >8.5.17, but next hard-pins an exact 8.4.31, so the highest we can hold via the overrides is 8.5.16 — still inside the advisory range. Build-time only (CSS processing over first-party stylesheets). WEAKEST justification in this file: revisit the moment next unpins 8.4.31.
